mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output, which improves corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases month-over-month but SPX 20-day return is negative in more than 40% of such months over a 2-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.0%, it signals significant labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and lowers corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad based demand weakness that depresses corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month
failure_condition: If simultaneous decline in HOUST and RSAFS is followed by positive SPX 60-day returns in more than half of events over a 3-year window, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations via higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If during periods of rising FEDFUNDS, SPX 5-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales reflects strong consumer spending, which consistently boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days across all regimes.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day return is negative in more than half of the months when RSAFS increases relative to the prior month, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1500 thousand units, it signals acute weakness in the housing market that spills over to the broader economy and depresses equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 1500, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment and decrease in the unemployment rate indicates exceptionally strong labor market conditions, which raises corporate profit expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease month-over-month
failure_condition: If in months with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE, SPX 20-day returns are negative more than 40% of the time, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate regime where the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, rising consumer price inflation signals healthy demand recovery without imminent tightening, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.5%, rising CPIAUCSL leads to negative 60-day returns in more than half of such episodes, hypothesis rejected.
